page: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores driven by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz with a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across memory and database operations. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth read-only tests for both small and large blocks, alongside strong large-block write performance. CPU execution is highly competitive, with top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database and in-memory operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations.

The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations. This hardware acceleration contributes to top-tier prompt processing speeds in LLM inference benchmarks across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds remain in the average to strong range. Given the high-density DDR5 memory, massive local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core compute, this instance is highly cost-efficient for resource-intensive workloads. It is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, big data analytics, and enterprise-grade workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 40 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET throughput. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier prompt processing for LLM inference. While text generation speeds are average, the instance offers excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe storage and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis SET performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration
Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory caching
